Re
Farn PolrrrcAl PRACTICES ConanatssloN
428 J Street r Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
December 6,2005
Heather K. Whitham
City of Torrance
Office of the City Attomey
3031 Torrance Blvd.
Torrance, CA 90503-5059
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. \M-05-215
Dear Ms. Witham:
This letter is in response to your request for reconsideration dated October 19,
2005. As stated in our initial response to your inquiry(W-05-163), because your inquiry
involves past conduct, we decline to provide you with written advice. (Regulation
r832e(bx8xA).)
Though the advice you seek may be at least partially relevant to future
contemplated acts of the Torrance Airport Commissioners, such prospective acts are
inextricably tied to past conduct (i.e., recommendations the commissioners have already
forwarded to the city council).
As you indicated in your original correspondence, the City of Torrance operates a
public airport on land owned by the city. ln connection with its airport operations, the
city leases land to a number of fixed base operators, private enterprises that provide such
aeronautical services as aircraft maintenance, fueling, flight haining and hangar and
aircraft tie-downs. The city itself also directly rents hangars and tie-downs.
City staff presented a proposed revision to the city's hangar rental agreement for
the Torrance Airport Commission's review and comments. The commission in tum
delegated the initial review of the proposed hangar rental agreement to an ad hoc
committee for its consideration.
The city council invites applications for commission appointment from Torrance
residents. Frequently the council appoints members of the public who have a particular
interest in the subject matter jurisdiction of a commission. As a result three current


File No. W-05-215
Page No. 2
Airport commissioners own planes and rent hangars from the city. Afourth
Commissioner owns an equity interest in a flying club based atTonanceAirport; he
apparerfily owns an undivided partial ownership interest in the club,s aircrafr.,which are
based atTonance Airport. The club also rents halgffspace attheairport form the city.
Both the ad hoc committee and the airport commission have considered the
proposed revisions to the city's hangar rental agreement. proposed revisions include
adding an automatic cost-of-living rent escalator, establishinjdifferent iui[urrental
rates for Torance residents and non-residents, heightened inJurance r"q,ri..?.rts and
broader indemnity requirements. The proposed rwisions will have an effect not only on
hangar rental rates but also on other costs of basing aircraft at the Torrance Airport.
The Airport Commission's comments and recorlmendations regarding the
proposed revision to the city's hangar rental agreement had, alreadybeJn foniarded to the
city council for its consideration at the time ofyour original correspondence. rn
reviewing matters brought before it, the city council refillarly, although nolalways,
accepts the recommendations of the city,s commissionJ.
Because your question is about the obligation of the Airport Commissioners, and
because they had already made recorlmendations and forward"d th"m to the city council
prior to your seeking advice on the Commissioners' behalf, we must decline yoor r.qr".t.
We again reiterate that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed
in your request andthat nothing in the Political Reform Act prevents an official from
seeking Tegal advice from you orprivate counsel.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
Andreas
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.conclusion: Because your question is about the obligation of the Airport Commissioners, and because they had already made recommendations and forwarded them to the city council prior to your seeking advice on the Commissioners' behalf, we must decline your request.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Airport Commissioners who own planes, rent hangars, or have equity interests in flying clubs have conflicts of interest in reviewing and recommending changes to the city's hangar rental agreement that would affect their own rental costs.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide written advice because the inquiry involves past conduct (recommendations already forwarded to city council) rather than prospective acts. The advice request cannot be separated from the past conduct already completed by the Commissioners.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter citing procedural basis (past conduct). No formal QUESTION section header present but legal issue is clear from context. Document references prior advice letter W-05-163 and request for reconsideration dated October 19, 2005.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Airport Commissioners who own planes, rent hangars, or have equity interests in flying clubs have conflicts of interest in reviewing and recommending changes to the city's hangar rental agreement that would affect their own rental costs.

CONCLUSION: Because your question is about the obligation of the Airport Commissioners, and because they had already made recommendations and forwarded them to the city council prior to your seeking advice on the Commissioners' behalf, we must decline your request.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines to provide written advice to the City of Torrance regarding potential conflicts of interest for Airport Commissioners in reviewing hangar rental agreement revisions, citing Regulation 1832e(b)(8)(A) because the inquiry involves past conduct and recommendations already forwarded to the city council.
